jokes.text: Eh, probably dead. Already dissolving in a bathtub, if we're lucky.
jokes.setup: Someone asks where the Professor is
jokes.punchline: Bender casually suggests he's dead and dissolving in a bathtub as the best-case scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - casually dark with efficient delivery. The 'if we're lucky' escalation is what elevates this above standard dark humor.

jokes.text: I remember it like it was interesting.
jokes.setup: Professor begins what sounds like a nostalgic reminiscence
jokes.punchline: Undercuts the expected 'like it was yesterday' with admitting it wasn't actually interesting
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth deadpan subversion. Economy is perfect - maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: I scoured the hardware store parking lot to assemble the finest crew imaginable.
jokes.setup: Professor describes assembling his crew with grandiose language
jokes.punchline: The grandiose 'finest crew imaginable' was assembled from a hardware store parking lot
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor contrasting high expectations with low-quality sourcing. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Candy, the raunchy by-the-books navigator
jokes.setup: Professor introduces his first crew member
jokes.punchline: 'Raunchy by-the-books' is a contradiction - you can't be both
jokes.explanation: Mild contradiction humor. The oxymoron works but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Lifter, the devastatingly handsome forklift
jokes.setup: Professor introduces another crew member
jokes.punchline: A forklift is described as 'devastatingly handsome' like a person
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor anthropomorphizing a machine with romantic language. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: Captain Lando Tucker, a dedicated young man with no characteristics.
jokes.setup: Professor introduces the captain with positive description
jokes.punchline: The positive trait is literally having no characteristics - a complete contradiction
jokes.explanation: Sharp contradiction humor that's more sophisticated than typical oxymorons. The setup-payoff structure works well.

jokes.text: And also Captain Tucker. Pow! Whoa! You totally got me, Dr. John. You're hilarious.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg greets his favorite patients enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Tucker is clearly not a favorite but plays along awkwardly with forced enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing the awkwardness of forced social interaction. Character-appropriate for both.

jokes.text: Zoidberg was popular? Zoidberg had hair?
jokes.setup: We see popular, hairy Zoidberg from the past
jokes.punchline: Fry's incredulous reaction highlights how pathetic current Zoidberg is
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor that plays off established Zoidberg traits. The double incredulity works well.

jokes.text: I never said he had hair! If you chose to imagine him that way, that's your business.
jokes.setup: Fry comments on Zoidberg having hair in the flashback
jokes.punchline: Professor deflects responsibility for his own visual storytelling onto the listener's imagination
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about visual storytelling in animation. The Professor's defensive deflection is both absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Ten... nine and so on. Three... two, one...
jokes.setup: Standard countdown sequence begins
jokes.punchline: Skips the entire middle of the countdown with 'and so on' - lazy incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of countdown expectations. Efficient way to show character's lack of dedication to details.

jokes.text: Go, you big metal bird. Get up there.
jokes.setup: Captain gives inspirational send-off to his ship
jokes.punchline: Extremely casual, unpoetic language for what should be a dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from underwhelming dramatic moment. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Zoidberg had friends?
jokes.setup: Flashback shows Zoidberg being popular and social
jokes.punchline: Amy's shock emphasizes how friendless present Zoidberg is
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback humor. Very economical delivery maximizes impact.

jokes.text: It was a different time.
jokes.setup: Multiple questions about how different past-Zoidberg was
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact explanation that completely fails to actually explain anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan non-explanation. The Professor's dismissive tone paired with complete lack of actual information is very funny.

jokes.text: Zoidberg had friends?
jokes.setup: Seeing Zoidberg waving goodbye to departing crew
jokes.punchline: Bender's disbelief that Zoidberg could have friends
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke about Zoidberg's unpopularity, reinforcing the theme

jokes.text: with only occasional breaks to tend to my grief diarrhea.
jokes.setup: Professor watched the skies for weeks in grief
jokes.punchline: The dignified vigil is undercut by having to deal with stress-induced digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of dramatic moment with gross-out bodily function. The contrast between noble vigil and digestive distress works well.

jokes.text: I thought you said he didn't have hair. Yes, but something he saw on that mission traumatized him so severely, he grew hair, just so it could turn white.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg appears with white hair despite Professor saying he had no hair
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation that trauma was so severe it made him grow hair specifically to turn white
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation. The logic is completely ridiculous but delivered with such confidence it becomes hilarious. Creative solution to visual continuity.

jokes.text: The Bermuda Tetrahedron is just a myth. I'm glad you're so brave in the face of the unknown, Leela, because, for your next mission, you'll be flying directly through the Bermuda Tetrahedron!
jokes.setup: Leela dismisses the Bermuda Tetrahedron as myth
jokes.punchline: Professor immediately assigns them to fly through it, making her bravado backfire
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with immediate consequences for character's statement. Well-structured but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Or we could fly around it. Of course you could. You'd be stupid not to.
jokes.setup: Professor assigns dangerous mission through Bermuda Tetrahedron
jokes.punchline: Casually admits the obvious safe alternative while calling them stupid for not taking it
jokes.explanation: Great Professor moment - simultaneously acknowledging the stupidity while forcing them to do it anyway. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: It shouldn't have an apostrophe. This means 'and it is crew.' What the hell's wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Monument has grammatical error
jokes.punchline: Leela's pedantic grammar correction with angry reaction to the mistake
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's perfectionism, but the grammar joke itself is fairly basic.

jokes.text: It's a minor error, lady. I mean, we're space aliens. It's a miracle we can even speak English.
jokes.setup: Leela complains about grammar error
jokes.punchline: Alien makes reasonable point about language barriers that highlights the absurdity of the show's premise
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-humor acknowledging the show's linguistic convenience. The alien makes a genuinely reasonable point.

jokes.text: The miracle is that I'm not kicking your ass.
jokes.setup: Alien mentions miracle of speaking English
jokes.punchline: Leela threatens violence, redefining what the real 'miracle' is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela response - tough, direct, and escalating the confrontation. Good callback structure to the 'miracle' word.

jokes.text: Uh-oh, the dials are terrified.
jokes.setup: Ship enters dangerous area
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing the instrument dials as having emotions
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with efficient delivery. The personification of instruments works well for establishing danger.

jokes.text: It's Tickle Me Elmo's Fire.
jokes.setup: Mysterious dangerous phenomenon in space
jokes.punchline: Parody of St. Elmo's Fire combining it with the annoying Tickle Me Elmo toy
jokes.explanation: Clever mashup of maritime phenomenon with annoying toy. Very economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Why did we have to come here at night?
jokes.setup: Crew discovers spooky spaceship graveyard
jokes.punchline: Amy complains about timing as if they had a choice about when to visit this random space location
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating space like a mundane location with visiting hours. Character-appropriate complaint.

jokes.text: There's the Garmin! And the TomTom!
jokes.setup: Looking at famous lost ships in the graveyard
jokes.punchline: GPS device brands treated as ship names, playing on how they help with navigation
jokes.explanation: Decent pun playing on navigation theme. Contemporary references work but aren't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Could it have been flavor-blasted?
jokes.setup: Examining damage to spaceship
jokes.punchline: Using snack food marketing term to describe ship damage
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment applying food terminology to serious situation. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The table is still set for a McDonald's value dinner.
jokes.setup: Crew left in a hurry
jokes.punchline: Evidence of their abandonment is a cheap fast food meal setting
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from contrasting dramatic abandonment with mundane fast food. Competent but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Such a meal to go to waste! And with that... I have closure.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg remembers traumatic event that gave him white hair
jokes.punchline: His trauma was just about wasted food, and he immediately gets closure from acknowledging it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his trauma is completely food-focused and he resolves it instantly. Great character integration and subversion of dramatic tension.

jokes.text: Hey, Zoidberg, you're cockatieling.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is presumably doing something bird-like
jokes.punchline: Bender uses 'cockatieling' as a verb for whatever visual behavior Zoidberg is exhibiting
jokes.explanation: Creative verb creation that's both economical and absurd. The made-up word perfectly captures the visual gag.

jokes.text: Holy crap, it's a giant space fish!
jokes.setup: Giant threatening creature appears
jokes.punchline: Fry misidentifies it in the most basic way possible
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about whale vs fish classification. Character-appropriate but simple.

jokes.text: Actually, the space whale isn't a space fish. It's a space mammal. Wow, interesting. I'm both impressed and being eaten.
jokes.setup: Pedantic correction about whale classification while being attacked
jokes.punchline: Fry's polite academic interest despite being consumed
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between educational moment and mortal peril. Fry's polite response while being eaten is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: I'm both impressed and being eaten.
jokes.setup: Leela giving scientific lecture while in danger
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of being eaten mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery of absurd situation, very economical

jokes.text: Like a millionaire on a cocktail wiener.
jokes.setup: Describing how fast the whale is moving
jokes.punchline: Bizarre simile comparing speed to wealthy person eating appetizers
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd simile. The imagery is both vivid and completely unexpected.

jokes.text: That's no ordinary space whale! It's a four-dimensional space whale!
jokes.setup: Professor identifies the threat level
jokes.punchline: Escalates from regular space whale to four-dimensional version
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation humor raising stakes to absurd sci-fi level. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Smeesh! He gonked off the engines.
jokes.setup: Whale damages the ship's engines
jokes.punchline: Amy uses made-up technical terms that sound appropriately mechanical
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Amy's casual made-up technical language. The invented words sound plausible.

jokes.text: I'm going after that Mobius Dick!
jokes.setup: Leela declares her obsession with hunting the whale
jokes.punchline: Combines Moby Dick reference with mathematical Mobius strip concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary reference combining Moby Dick with mathematical concept. Perfect for sci-fi setting and highly quotable. Shows excellent writing craft.

jokes.text: You've been hitting the Red Stripe, woman!
jokes.setup: Leela's obsessive behavior
jokes.punchline: Bender accuses her of drinking specific beer brand as explanation for erratic behavior
jokes.explanation: Brand name humor that's character-appropriate for Bender but not particularly clever. The specific beer choice is arbitrary.

jokes.text: I could marry you and Bender against your will, if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: Leela asserts captain's authority at sea
jokes.punchline: Threatens forced marriage as example of absolute power
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on maritime authority. The threat is both specific and completely arbitrary.

jokes.text: You wouldn't dare! I've been married to worse.
jokes.setup: Fry protests the forced marriage threat
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact response that he's had worse spouses
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Bender's casual acceptance of bizarre situations. The implication about his romantic history is funny.

jokes.text: I'm not doctor, but I'm afraid you be exhibiting symptoms of illin'.
jokes.setup: Hermes diagnoses Leela's obsessive behavior
jokes.punchline: Medical terminology mixed with slang 'illin'' and grammatical errors
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay mixing formal medical language with street slang. Character-appropriate for Hermes's bureaucratic nature.

jokes.text: You've gone from crazy like a fox to crazy like Fox News.
jokes.setup: Describing escalation of Leela's mental state
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'crazy like a fox' idiom, escalating to Fox News network
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay escalating from clever craziness to destructive craziness. Very economical and highly quotable political humor.

jokes.text: In my dreams, I've peered beyond its eyes and into the cackling hell within us all.
jokes.setup: Fry supposedly offers wisdom about the whale
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly dark and poetic philosophical statement from usually simple Fry
jokes.explanation: Unexpected philosophical depth from Fry creates good contrast with his usual simplicity. The language is appropriately grandiose.

jokes.text: Fry's right! Let's kill the captain and order some strippers!
jokes.setup: Fry makes profound statement about inner darkness
jokes.punchline: Bender interprets it as excuse for mutiny and debauchery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response taking philosophical insight and immediately applying it to selfish desires. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Things look bad enough without having to look closer at them.
jokes.setup: Examining their dire situation
jokes.punchline: Bender's philosophy of willful ignorance as coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical observation delivered as practical advice. Very economical and quotable wisdom.

jokes.text: I spent a semester in Africa harpooning giraffes. And giraffes are basically just land space whales.
jokes.setup: Amy volunteers for harpooning duty
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous qualification combining animal cruelty with nonsensical taxonomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic chain that's both horrible and hilarious. The classification system is completely nonsensical but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I'm having a Serengeti flashback. Die, you dirty giraffe!
jokes.setup: Amy prepares to harpoon the whale
jokes.punchline: Flashback to her giraffe hunting days with violent outburst
jokes.explanation: Good callback that develops Amy's dark past. The violent outburst contrasts well with her usual personality.

jokes.text: I guess I thought whales died when you harpooned them.
jokes.setup: Whale survives being harpooned
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact admission that she hadn't thought this through
jokes.explanation: Good moment of captain incompetence. The casual admission of poor planning is appropriately understated.

jokes.text: I can see sideways in time.
jokes.setup: Ship is being pulled through fourth dimension
jokes.punchline: Bender's poetic description of experiencing higher dimensions
jokes.explanation: Beautiful poetic description of experiencing fourth dimension. Very economical and evocative language.

jokes.text: Hey, I see CGI.
jokes.setup: Bender continues describing what he sees in fourth dimension
jokes.punchline: Breaks fourth wall by acknowledging the show's animation
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about animation production. Extremely economical delivery with perfect timing after the poetic previous line.

jokes.text: That was the greatest unaccountably infinite bunch of guys I ever met.
jokes.setup: Bender returns from fourth dimensional experience
jokes.punchline: Describes meeting infinite versions of himself with characteristic self-love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment combining sci-fi concept with his narcissism. The phrase 'unaccountably infinite' is particularly good.

jokes.text: Stupid rocks. Think they're so great. Well, they are giant diamonds.
jokes.setup: Bender complains about the rocks they're hitting
jokes.punchline: His complaints are immediately undercut when he realizes they're valuable diamonds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's instant change from hostility to greed. The structure works well.

jokes.text: A lot of Bender fell out of the crow's nest.
jokes.setup: Bender presumably loses parts of himself
jokes.punchline: Treating Bender as both a person and a quantity of material
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay treating robot character as both individual and material. Economical and appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Well, they are giant diamonds.
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about rocks
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're actually incredibly valuable
jokes.explanation: Good reversal that makes Bender's complaint ironic

jokes.text: Enough of your mad obsession with Bender, Fry.
jokes.setup: Fry wants to save Bender
jokes.punchline: Leela accuses Fry of obsession while being completely obsessed herself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Leela's complete lack of self-awareness about her own obsession. Character-appropriate hypocrisy.

jokes.text: Aw, so close.
jokes.setup: Leela attempts to perform wedding ceremony but is interrupted
jokes.punchline: Bender's disappointed reaction to missing the forced wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender reaction showing he was actually looking forward to the absurd wedding. Very economical callback.

jokes.text: That's enough out of me.
jokes.setup: Leela arguing with herself as captain and crew
jokes.punchline: Tells herself to stop talking
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment showing Leela's complete mental break through self-contradiction

jokes.text: With my Oxo Good Grips Cheese Knife, I stab at thee.
jokes.setup: Leela attempts dramatic confrontation with whale
jokes.punchline: Epic Moby Dick reference using mundane kitchen utensil with brand name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic literary moment with specific product placement. The contrast between epic language and mundane tool is perfect.

jokes.text: Not to mention those single socks from the dryer, am I right?
jokes.setup: Professor mourns crews lost to Bermuda Tetrahedron
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg blames it for common household mystery
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor connecting cosmic mystery to mundane annoyance. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Like that Bible guy who got swallowed by the whale-- Pinocchio.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to make Biblical reference about being in whale's belly
jokes.punchline: Confuses Jonah with Pinocchio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry mistake mixing up religious and children's stories. Character-appropriate cultural confusion.

jokes.text: For the last time, I'm not obsessed. I just want this whale to die, die, die!
jokes.setup: Leela denies being obsessed with the whale
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves obsession with increasingly violent repetition
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic escalation showing complete lack of self-awareness. The 'die, die, die' perfectly undermines her denial.

jokes.text: Holy crap! Four dimensional bowels! Einstein was right.
jokes.setup: They're being digested by the four-dimensional whale
jokes.punchline: Connects theoretical physics to digestive system
jokes.explanation: Good combination of high science concept with gross bodily function. The Einstein reference adds academic credibility to the absurd.

jokes.text: Why?! Why?! Also what and how?!
jokes.setup: Fry faces existential crisis in whale's stomach
jokes.punchline: Expands from emotional anguish to practical information requests
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from philosophical despair to practical curiosity. Very economical structure that captures Fry's character well.

jokes.text: It's a great mammal. See? It's got whiskers. And I saw it lactating earlier.
jokes.setup: Continuing whale vs fish classification debate
jokes.punchline: Bender provides disturbing evidence of whale's mammalian nature
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag with increasingly uncomfortable details. The lactation observation is appropriately disturbing.

jokes.text: Wow. Are you gross looking.
jokes.setup: Amy meets the transformed Captain Tucker
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy moment with characteristically blunt honesty. Very economical and well-delivered insult.

jokes.text: The great space serpent... Mammal.
jokes.setup: Tucker calls it a serpent
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction from the group maintaining taxonomic accuracy
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag efficiently. The immediate correction shows the group's dedication to proper classification.

jokes.text: I am the whale!
jokes.setup: Leela's obsession reaches its peak
jokes.punchline: Ultimate identification with her target, echoing Ahab's final moments
jokes.explanation: Powerful climactic moment perfectly echoing Moby Dick themes. Maximum economy with tremendous dramatic impact. Excellent character arc completion.

jokes.text: Popsicles here. Can't mourn the dead without a Popsicle. You think a Popsicle gonna bring my daughter back? No, sir. Aha! You just talked yourself out of a sale.
jokes.setup: Popsicle vendor tries to sell at funeral with emotional appeal
jokes.punchline: Mourner's logical response backfires on vendor's sales technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd sales interaction at inappropriate venue. The vendor's twisted logic and customer's reasonable response create perfect comedy.

jokes.text: My days of joy and luck are over. Guess I got to quit that club.
jokes.setup: Hermes mourns Amy's apparent death
jokes.punchline: Treats grief practically by canceling membership in Joy and Luck club
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes bureaucratic approach to emotion. The practical response to grief is both absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You always think you're going to disappear in space before your children.
jokes.setup: Professor expresses paternal grief
jokes.punchline: Inverts normal parent/child mortality expectations for space setting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures the dangers of space travel while subverting normal mortality expectations. Very clever writing.

jokes.text: Oh, no. My deal is doing the thing.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's body starts reacting to something
jokes.punchline: Vague, panicked description of biological process
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment with characteristically imprecise medical terminology. The vagueness adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Oy, now with the horror hair.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's hair turns white from trauma again
jokes.punchline: Yiddish-inflected complaint about his recurring stress response
jokes.explanation: Good callback with characteristically Jewish phrasing from Zoidberg. The complaint format works well.

jokes.text: Popsicles. Fill your gaping mouth with a Popsicle.
jokes.setup: Vendor continues sales pitch during chaos
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately aggressive sales technique with unappetizing imagery
jokes.explanation: Continuing the inappropriate sales pitch character with gross imagery. The persistence is both absurd and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: That sounds clever, but it doesn't explain much.
jokes.setup: Leela gives poetic explanation of her transformation
jokes.punchline: Bender points out that the explanation is style over substance
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on dramatic exposition. Bender cuts through pretentious explanation with practical criticism.

jokes.text: I'm 61. I don't know him. What else you got?
jokes.setup: Lando returns after 50 years to reunite with son
jokes.punchline: Son coldly rejects father and treats it like shopping for replacement relatives
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark rejection of sentimental reunion. The consumer mentality applied to family relationships is perfect dark humor.

jokes.text: I don't know him. What else you got?
jokes.setup: Father returning after 50 years
jokes.punchline: Son completely rejects him like shopping for alternatives
jokes.explanation: Brutally casual rejection of parent, treating father like vendor

jokes.text: The whale must have some kind of Mobius colon that endlessly recycles time and space. Yep. That stands up to scrutiny.
jokes.setup: Professor explains how crew didn't age
jokes.punchline: Completely ridiculous pseudo-scientific explanation accepted without question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pseudo-science combining mathematical concept with digestive system. Bender's matter-of-fact acceptance makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: On the other hand, the stupid fish did eat us.
jokes.setup: Leela gives philosophical speech about not blaming the whale
jokes.punchline: Bender provides practical counterargument while using wrong classification
jokes.explanation: Good practical counterargument to philosophical wisdom. Continues classification running gag while providing logical perspective.

jokes.text: Yep. That stands up to scrutiny.
jokes.setup: Professor's absurd scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Leela accepts completely nonsensical theory
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan acceptance of ridiculous pseudo-science
